tenex-edge: tmux_spawn RPC: daemon error [rpc_error]: no harness command for agent "writer": add a "command" field to ~/.tenex/edge/agents/writer.json


yet the file already has the command

Two fixes applied to `~/.tenex/edge/agents/writer.json`:

1. **`command` field was a string** — `serde_json` deserializes `Option<Vec<String>>` and silently returns `None` for a plain string, so `resolve_agent_command` fell through to `SPAWN_DEFS` (which has no entry for "writer") and errored.
2. **`~` in the path** — the command is passed directly to `tmux new-session --` without a shell, so tilde wouldn't expand. Changed to the absolute path.

The file now has:
```json
"command": ["claude", "--dangerously-skip-permissions", "--agent", "/Users/pablofernandez/.tenex/edge/agents-definition/writer.json"]
```

`tenex-edge launch writer` should work now. If you want `~` to work in agent files generally (it's more portable), I can add a tilde-expansion pass in `resolve_agent_command`.

actually it doesn't work like this, at least with claude code

let's put on the agents/slug.json file a agent: { json-goes-here }

then we'll need a per-harness translator that translates how to pass that in

in the case of claude it's using --agents { slug: json-here } --agent slug

for example, with the current files we have this works:

claude --agents "$(jq -c '{writer: {description: .description, prompt: .system_prompt}}' ~/.tenex/edge/agents-definition/writer.json)" --agent writer

and launches the writer agent properly in claude code


so command: [ "claude", "--dangerously-skip-permissions" ]

with the agent: { "name": "Writer", "system_prompt": .... } would get expanded to claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --agents { "writer": { "name": .... } --agent writer

Session is up. Summary of what was built:

- **`StoredKey`** now has `agent: Option<serde_json::Value>` — the inline agent definition stored directly in the identity file
- **`list_local_agents`** returns a 3-tuple `(slug, command, agent_def)` 
- **`resolve_spawn_entry`** replaces `resolve_agent_command`, returning `(base_cmd, agent_def)`
- **`apply_agent_def_args`** is the per-harness translator — for `claude` binary it wraps the def as `{"<slug>": <def>}` and appends `--agents '<json>' --agent <slug>`; other harnesses are a no-op for now
- `spawn_agent` applies the translation; `resume_agent` uses the base command only (resume already has the agent definition from the session)
- **`writer.json`** now has `command: ["claude", "--dangerously-skip-permissions"]` + `agent: {description, prompt}` — no more external file reference
